2603.19539.title: FDARxBench: Benchmarking Regulatory and Clinical Reasoning on FDA Generic Drug Assessment
2603.19539.paper_summary: We introduce an expert curated, real-world benchmark for evaluating document-grounded question-answering (QA) motivated by generic drug assessment, using the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) drug label documents. Drug labels contain rich but heterogeneous clinical and regulatory information, making accurate question answering difficult for current language models. In collaboration with FDA regulatory assessors, we introduce FDARxBench, and construct a multi-stage pipeline for generating high-quality, expert curated, QA examples spanning factual, multi-hop, and refusal tasks, and design evaluation protocols to assess both open-book and closed-book reasoning. Experiments across proprietary and open-weight models reveal substantial gaps in factual grounding, long-context retrieval, and safe refusal behavior. While motivated by FDA generic drug assessment needs, this benchmark also provides a substantial foundation for challenging regulatory-grade evaluation of label comprehension. The benchmark is designed to support evaluation of LLM behavior on drug-label questions.
2603.19539.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了一個專家策劃的、針對文件基礎問題回答（QA）的現實基準，這是受到美國食品藥品監督管理局（FDA）藥物標籤文件的啟發。藥物標籤包含豐富但異質的臨床和監管信息，這使得當前語言模型的準確問題回答變得困難。與FDA的監管評估員合作，我們推出了FDARxBench，並構建了一個多階段管道，用於生成高質量的、專家策劃的QA示例，涵蓋事實性、多跳和拒絕任務，並設計評估協議以評估開放書籍和封閉書籍的推理。對專有和開放權重模型的實驗顯示，在事實基礎、長上下文檢索和安全拒絕行為方面存在顯著差距。雖然受到FDA通用藥物評估需求的驅動，但這個基準也為挑戰性的監管級別標籤理解評估提供了實質基礎。該基準旨在支持對藥物標籤問題的LLM行為評估。
2603.19539.author: Betty Xiong et.al.
2603.19539.authors: Betty Xiong, Jillian Fisher, Benjamin Newman, Meng Hu, Shivangi Gupta, Yejin Choi, Lanyan Fang, Russ B Altman
